input.purpose: Editorial briefing for a consumer technology magazine team
input.raw_material: Right to repair laws require manufacturers to make spare parts, tools, and repair manuals available to consumers and independent repair shops. The EU passed a right to repair directive in 2024. Several US states have passed their own laws. Apple and Samsung have started self-repair programmes. Independent repair shops say manufacturers still make it too difficult. E-waste is a driver - 50 million tonnes generated globally per year. Average smartphone lifespan is 2.5 years but could be 5+ with repairs.

input.purpose: Briefing for a local TV news team
input.raw_material: Three candidates: current deputy mayor, a local business owner, and a community organiser. Election is April 15. Key issues: housing costs, public transport, and crime. Last election turnout was 32%.

input.topic: The European Health Data Space regulation
input.purpose: Production reference for a documentary team filming episodes about digital health across Europe
input.target_audience: Documentary producers and directors with limited health policy knowledge
input.raw_material: EHDS proposed by European Commission in May 2022. Aims to create a single market for health data across the EU. Two components: primary use (patients access their own records across borders) and secondary use (anonymised health data available for research). Adopted by European Parliament in 2024. Must be transposed by member states. Concerns about data privacy, especially in Germany where health data sensitivity is high. Potential benefits for rare disease research. Pharmaceutical industry supports secondary use provisions. Patient groups are divided - some see research benefits, others fear commercial exploitation.
